response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong full-stack, DLang, and some C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, bachelor thesis), plus relevant geospatial experience through GISCollective. However, the role demands modern C++ or Rust as a primary daily skill, and the resume shows no recent commercial C++/Rust work (last C/C++ role was 2015) and no distributed-systems or routing/tiling pipeline experience. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to a strong generalist willing to ramp up on C++.
strengths: Geospatial domain experience via GISCollective (GIS platform, Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of req/s)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testability, open-source contributions
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust production experience (C/C++ last used ~2015 at NI)
Primary stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang, not aligned with C++ routing pipeline work
No demonstrated distributed-systems or navigation/routing tile pipeline background

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++/Rust experience
